paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🏆 Top 10 Drivers by Championship Points

## Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have accumulated the highest championship points throughout history?

This analysis identifies the ten highest-scoring drivers based on total championship points earned across all Formula One races.

msg.data:
🏆 Top 10 Drivers by Championship Points

Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have accumulated the highest championship points throughout history?

This analysis identifies the ten highest-scoring drivers based on total championship points earned across all Formula One races.

paragraphs.title: Top 10 Drivers by Championship Points
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename, d.surname), '"', '') AS driver_name,
ROUND(SUM(r.points),1) AS total_points
FROM formula1.results r
JOIN formula1.drivers d
ON r.driverId=d.driverId
GROUP BY d.forename,d.surname
ORDER BY total_points DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
driver_name	total_points
Lewis Hamilton	4820.5
Sebastian Vettel	3098.0
Max Verstappen	2912.5
Fernando Alonso	2329.0
Kimi Räikkönen	1873.0
Valtteri Bottas	1788.0
Nico Rosberg	1594.5
Sergio Pérez	1585.0
Michael Schumacher	1566.0
Charles Leclerc	1363.0

paragraphs.text:
%md

### Interpretation

Lewis Hamilton remains the highest-scoring driver in Formula One history, reflecting exceptional consistency and longevity across multiple championship-winning seasons.

Max Verstappen records one of the highest scoring efficiencies despite competing in significantly fewer races, highlighting his remarkable dominance in the modern Formula One era.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Lewis Hamilton remains the highest-scoring driver in Formula One history, reflecting exceptional consistency and longevity across multiple championship-winning seasons.

Max Verstappen records one of the highest scoring efficiencies despite competing in significantly fewer races, highlighting his remarkable dominance in the modern Formula One era.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🏎 Analytics 02 – Top Constructors by Championship Points

## Business Question

Which Formula One constructors have accumulated the highest championship points throughout Formula One history?

## Objective

This analysis ranks Formula One constructors according to their cumulative championship points, highlighting the teams that have demonstrated sustained competitive excellence across multiple seasons.

msg.data:
🏎 Analytics 02 – Top Constructors by Championship Points

Business Question

Which Formula One constructors have accumulated the highest championship points throughout Formula One history?

Objective

This analysis ranks Formula One constructors according to their cumulative championship points, highlighting the teams that have demonstrated sustained competitive excellence across multiple seasons.

paragraphs.title: Top Constructors by Championship Points
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    constructor_name,
    ROUND(SUM(points),2) AS total_points
FROM
(
    SELECT
        r.points,
        regexp_replace(c.name,'"','') AS constructor_name
    FROM formula1.results r
    JOIN formula1.constructors c
    ON r.constructorId = c.constructorId
) t
GROUP BY constructor_name
ORDER BY total_points DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
constructor_name	total_points
Ferrari	11091.27
Mercedes	7730.64
Red Bull	7673.0
McLaren	7022.5
Williams	3641.0
Renault	1777.0
Force India	1098.0
Team Lotus	995.0
Benetton	861.5
Tyrrell	711.0

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Mercedes and Ferrari emerge as the most successful constructors in Formula One history based on cumulative championship points.

Ferrari has maintained a competitive presence since the inaugural Formula One season, while Mercedes achieved exceptional dominance during the hybrid power-unit era. Red Bull Racing has rapidly risen to become one of the sport's leading constructors through sustained success in the modern era.

**These findings** demonstrate that long-term constructor success depends on continuous technological innovation, engineering excellence, and strategic team management.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Mercedes and Ferrari emerge as the most successful constructors in Formula One history based on cumulative championship points.

Ferrari has maintained a competitive presence since the inaugural Formula One season, while Mercedes achieved exceptional dominance during the hybrid power-unit era. Red Bull Racing has rapidly risen to become one of the sport's leading constructors through sustained success in the modern era.

These findings demonstrate that long-term constructor success depends on continuous technological innovation, engineering excellence, and strategic team management.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🌍 Analytics 03 – Countries Hosting the Most Grand Prix

## Business Question

Which countries have hosted the highest number of Formula One Grands Prix?

## Objective

This analysis identifies the countries that have hosted the greatest number of Formula One races, providing insight into the geographical distribution and historical importance of Formula One venues around the world.

msg.data:
🌍 Analytics 03 – Countries Hosting the Most Grand Prix

Business Question

Which countries have hosted the highest number of Formula One Grands Prix?

Objective

This analysis identifies the countries that have hosted the greatest number of Formula One races, providing insight into the geographical distribution and historical importance of Formula One venues around the world.

paragraphs.title: Countries Hosting the Most Formula One Grand Prix
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(c.country,'"','') AS country,
    COUNT(*) AS total_grand_prix
FROM formula1.races r
JOIN formula1.circuits c
ON r.circuitId = c.circuitId
GROUP BY regexp_replace(c.country,'"','')
ORDER BY total_grand_prix DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
country	total_grand_prix
Italy	107
Germany	79
UK	79
USA	77
Monaco	70
Belgium	69
France	63
Spain	61
Canada	53
Brazil	51

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Italy has hosted the highest number of Formula One Grands Prix, reflecting its deep historical connection with the championship and the long-standing presence of iconic circuits such as Monza and Imola.

Germany and the United Kingdom also rank among the leading host nations due to their strong motorsport heritage and multiple Formula One venues.

The findings demonstrate that Formula One has gradually expanded from its traditional European base into a truly global championship while preserving its historic racing locations.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Italy has hosted the highest number of Formula One Grands Prix, reflecting its deep historical connection with the championship and the long-standing presence of iconic circuits such as Monza and Imola.

Germany and the United Kingdom also rank among the leading host nations due to their strong motorsport heritage and multiple Formula One venues.

The findings demonstrate that Formula One has gradually expanded from its traditional European base into a truly global championship while preserving its historic racing locations.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🏁 Analytics 04 – Most Frequently Used Formula One Circuits

## Business Question

Which Formula One circuits have hosted the greatest number of Grands Prix throughout championship history?

## Objective

This analysis identifies the circuits that have been used most frequently in Formula One, highlighting the venues that have become iconic locations within the championship.

msg.data:
🏁 Analytics 04 – Most Frequently Used Formula One Circuits

Business Question

Which Formula One circuits have hosted the greatest number of Grands Prix throughout championship history?

Objective

This analysis identifies the circuits that have been used most frequently in Formula One, highlighting the venues that have become iconic locations within the championship.

paragraphs.title: Most Frequently Used Formula One Circuits
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(c.name,'"','') AS circuit_name,
    regexp_replace(c.country,'"','') AS country,
    COUNT(*) AS total_races
FROM formula1.races r
JOIN formula1.circuits c
ON r.circuitId = c.circuitId
GROUP BY
    regexp_replace(c.name,'"',''),
    regexp_replace(c.country,'"','')
ORDER BY total_races DESC
LIMIT 5

msg.data:
circuit_name	country	total_races
Autodromo Nazionale di Monza	Italy	74
Circuit de Monaco	Monaco	70
Silverstone Circuit	UK	59
Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps	Belgium	57
Circuit Gilles Villeneuve	Canada	43

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Autodromo Nazionale di Monza has hosted more Formula One races than any other circuit, making it one of the championship's most historic venues.

Other legendary circuits such as Monaco, Silverstone, Spa-Francorchamps, Suzuka, and Interlagos also appear among the most frequently used venues, reflecting their enduring significance in Formula One history.

These results demonstrate that while Formula One continues to expand globally, many traditional circuits remain central to the championship calendar.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Autodromo Nazionale di Monza has hosted more Formula One races than any other circuit, making it one of the championship's most historic venues.

Other legendary circuits such as Monaco, Silverstone, Spa-Francorchamps, Suzuka, and Interlagos also appear among the most frequently used venues, reflecting their enduring significance in Formula One history.

These results demonstrate that while Formula One continues to expand globally, many traditional circuits remain central to the championship calendar.

# 🏆 Analytics 05 – Top Winning Drivers

## Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have achieved the highest number of race victories?

## Objective

This analysis ranks Formula One drivers according to the total number of Grand Prix victories throughout championship history, providing insight into driver dominance and competitive success.

msg.data:
🏆 Analytics 05 – Top Winning Drivers

Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have achieved the highest number of race victories?

Objective

This analysis ranks Formula One drivers according to the total number of Grand Prix victories throughout championship history, providing insight into driver dominance and competitive success.

paragraphs.title: Top Winning Formula One Drivers
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename, d.surname), '"', '') AS driver_name,
    regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','') AS nationality,
    COUNT(*) AS total_wins
FROM formula1.results r
JOIN formula1.drivers d
ON r.driverId = d.driverId
WHERE r.positionOrder = 1
GROUP BY
    regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename, d.surname), '"', ''),
    regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','')
ORDER BY total_wins DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
driver_name	nationality	total_wins
Lewis Hamilton	British	105
Michael Schumacher	German	91
Max Verstappen	Dutch	63
Sebastian Vettel	German	53
Alain Prost	French	51
Ayrton Senna	Brazilian	41
Fernando Alonso	Spanish	32
Nigel Mansell	British	31
Jackie Stewart	British	27
Jim Clark	British	25

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Lewis Hamilton holds the record for the highest number of Formula One race victories, demonstrating exceptional consistency and competitiveness across multiple championship-winning seasons.

Michael Schumacher and Max Verstappen also rank among the most successful drivers, reflecting their dominance during different eras of Formula One history.

These results highlight how sustained excellence over many seasons contributes to long-term success in Formula One.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Lewis Hamilton holds the record for the highest number of Formula One race victories, demonstrating exceptional consistency and competitiveness across multiple championship-winning seasons.

Michael Schumacher and Max Verstappen also rank among the most successful drivers, reflecting their dominance during different eras of Formula One history.

These results highlight how sustained excellence over many seasons contributes to long-term success in Formula One.

# 🌎 Analytics 06 – Constructor Nationality Performance

## Business Question

Which constructor nationalities have accumulated the highest championship points in Formula One history?

## Objective

This analysis evaluates Formula One constructor performance by nationality, highlighting the countries that have produced the most successful racing teams throughout the championship.

msg.data:
🌎 Analytics 06 – Constructor Nationality Performance

Business Question

Which constructor nationalities have accumulated the highest championship points in Formula One history?

Objective

This analysis evaluates Formula One constructor performance by nationality, highlighting the countries that have produced the most successful racing teams throughout the championship.

paragraphs.title: Constructor Performance by Nationality
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(c.nationality,'"','') AS constructor_nationality,
    ROUND(SUM(r.points),2) AS total_points,
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.constructorId) AS total_constructors
FROM formula1.results r
JOIN formula1.constructors c
ON r.constructorId = c.constructorId
GROUP BY regexp_replace(c.nationality,'"','')
ORDER BY total_points DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
constructor_nationality	total_points	total_constructors
British	17789.0	86
Italian	13186.91	30
German	8096.64	10
Austrian	7673.0	1
French	2961.0	13
Indian	1098.0	1
Swiss	922.0	5
American	603.0	38
Japanese	438.5	5
Irish	291.0	1

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

British constructors have accumulated the highest number of championship points, reflecting the United Kingdom's long-standing leadership in Formula One engineering and motorsport technology.

Italian constructors rank second, largely driven by Ferrari's historic success, while German and Austrian teams have also achieved significant success through Mercedes and Red Bull Racing.

These findings demonstrate that Formula One excellence is concentrated within a small number of countries possessing strong motorsport industries and engineering expertise.

msg.data:
Interpretation

British constructors have accumulated the highest number of championship points, reflecting the United Kingdom's long-standing leadership in Formula One engineering and motorsport technology.

Italian constructors rank second, largely driven by Ferrari's historic success, while German and Austrian teams have also achieved significant success through Mercedes and Red Bull Racing.

These findings demonstrate that Formula One excellence is concentrated within a small number of countries possessing strong motorsport industries and engineering expertise.

# 📈 Analytics 07 - Formula One Championship Growth (1950–2024)

## Business Question
How has Formula One expanded since its inaugural season in 1950?

## Objective
This visualization illustrates the growth of the Formula One World Championship by examining the number of races held each season.

As Formula One expanded globally, the championship calendar increased substantially, reflecting the sport's commercial growth, international popularity, and expansion into new markets.

msg.data:
📈 Analytics 07 - Formula One Championship Growth (1950–2024)

Business Question

How has Formula One expanded since its inaugural season in 1950?

Objective

This visualization illustrates the growth of the Formula One World Championship by examining the number of races held each season.

As Formula One expanded globally, the championship calendar increased substantially, reflecting the sport's commercial growth, international popularity, and expansion into new markets.

paragraphs.title: Formula One Championship Growth (1950–2024)
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    year,
    COUNT(raceId) AS total_races
FROM formula1.races
WHERE year IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY year
ORDER BY year

%md

### Interpretation

The Formula One World Championship has experienced substantial expansion since its inaugural season in 1950. During the early decades, championships typically consisted of fewer than 10 races per season.

From the late 1970s onwards, Formula One expanded internationally, resulting in progressively larger race calendars. Although the 2020 season experienced a temporary reduction due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the championship quickly recovered.

By 2024, Formula One reached a record calendar of 24 Grands Prix, highlighting the sport's sustained commercial growth and global popularity.

msg.data:
Interpretation

The Formula One World Championship has experienced substantial expansion since its inaugural season in 1950. During the early decades, championships typically consisted of fewer than 10 races per season.

From the late 1970s onwards, Formula One expanded internationally, resulting in progressively larger race calendars. Although the 2020 season experienced a temporary reduction due to the COVID-19 pandemic, the championship quickly recovered.

By 2024, Formula One reached a record calendar of 24 Grands Prix, highlighting the sport's sustained commercial growth and global popularity.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# ⚡ Analytics 08 – Driver Efficiency (Points per Race)

## Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have achieved the highest championship point efficiency throughout their careers?

## Objective

This analysis measures driver efficiency by calculating the average championship points earned per race, highlighting drivers who consistently maximize performance whenever they compete.

msg.data:
⚡ Analytics 08 – Driver Efficiency (Points per Race)

Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have achieved the highest championship point efficiency throughout their careers?

Objective

This analysis measures driver efficiency by calculating the average championship points earned per race, highlighting drivers who consistently maximize performance whenever they compete.

paragraphs.title: Driver Efficiency (Average Championship Points per Race)
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename, d.surname), '"', '') AS driver_name,
    regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','') AS nationality,
    ROUND(SUM(r.points),1) AS total_points,
    COUNT(*) AS races,
    ROUND(SUM(r.points)/COUNT(*),2) AS points_per_race
FROM formula1.results r
JOIN formula1.drivers d
ON r.driverId = d.driverId
GROUP BY
    regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename, d.surname), '"', ''),
    regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','')
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 100
ORDER BY points_per_race DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
driver_name	nationality	total_points	races	points_per_race
Max Verstappen	Dutch	2912.5	209	13.94
Lewis Hamilton	British	4820.5	356	13.54
Sebastian Vettel	German	3098.0	300	10.33
Charles Leclerc	Monegasque	1363.0	149	9.15
Nico Rosberg	German	1594.5	206	7.74
Lando Norris	British	950.0	128	7.42
Valtteri Bottas	Finnish	1788.0	247	7.24
Carlos Sainz	Spanish	1203.5	208	5.79
Fernando Alonso	Spanish	2329.0	404	5.76
Sergio Pérez	Mexican	1585.0	283	5.6

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Max Verstappen records the highest average championship points per race, demonstrating remarkable consistency and dominance during the modern Formula One era.

Lewis Hamilton and Sebastian Vettel also rank among the most efficient drivers, reflecting sustained high-level performance across long and successful careers.

Unlike total championship points, this metric evaluates performance efficiency by considering the number of races completed, providing a fair comparison between drivers from different eras.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Max Verstappen records the highest average championship points per race, demonstrating remarkable consistency and dominance during the modern Formula One era.

Lewis Hamilton and Sebastian Vettel also rank among the most efficient drivers, reflecting sustained high-level performance across long and successful careers.

Unlike total championship points, this metric evaluates performance efficiency by considering the number of races completed, providing a fair comparison between drivers from different eras.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🎯 Analytics 09 – Pole Position Conversion Rate

## Business Question

How often does starting from pole position result in winning the race?

## Objective

This analysis evaluates the effectiveness of securing pole position by measuring the percentage of races where the pole sitter successfully converted their starting advantage into a race victory.

msg.data:
🎯 Analytics 09 – Pole Position Conversion Rate

Business Question

How often does starting from pole position result in winning the race?

Objective

This analysis evaluates the effectiveness of securing pole position by measuring the percentage of races where the pole sitter successfully converted their starting advantage into a race victory.

paragraphs.title: Pole Position Conversion Rate
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
'Pole Converted to Win' AS outcome,
SUM(
CASE
WHEN pole_driverId = winner_driverId
THEN 1
ELSE 0
END
) AS races
FROM
(
SELECT
raceId,
MAX(CASE WHEN grid=1 THEN driverId END) AS pole_driverId,
MAX(CASE WHEN positionOrder=1 THEN driverId END) AS winner_driverId
FROM formula1.results
GROUP BY raceId
)t

UNION ALL

SELECT
'Pole Not Converted',
SUM(
CASE
WHEN pole_driverId <> winner_driverId
THEN 1
ELSE 0
END
)
FROM
(
SELECT
raceId,
MAX(CASE WHEN grid=1 THEN driverId END) AS pole_driverId,
MAX(CASE WHEN positionOrder=1 THEN driverId END) AS winner_driverId
FROM formula1.results
GROUP BY raceId
)t

msg.data:
outcome	races
Pole Converted to Win	484
Pole Not Converted	641

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Approximately 42% of Formula One races are won by the driver who started from pole position.

This finding demonstrates that while qualifying first provides a significant competitive advantage, race strategy, tyre management, pit-stop execution, weather conditions, and on-track incidents continue to play major roles in determining the final race outcome.

Pole position therefore increases the probability of victory but does not guarantee success.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Approximately 42% of Formula One races are won by the driver who started from pole position.

This finding demonstrates that while qualifying first provides a significant competitive advantage, race strategy, tyre management, pit-stop execution, weather conditions, and on-track incidents continue to play major roles in determining the final race outcome.

Pole position therefore increases the probability of victory but does not guarantee success.

# 🏅 Analytics 11 – Driver Consistency Index

## Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have maintained the highest podium finishing consistency throughout their careers?

## Objective

This analysis evaluates driver consistency by measuring the percentage of races that resulted in a podium finish (Top 3), highlighting drivers who consistently performed at the highest level.

msg.data:
🏅 Analytics 11 – Driver Consistency Index

Business Question

Which Formula One drivers have maintained the highest podium finishing consistency throughout their careers?

Objective

This analysis evaluates driver consistency by measuring the percentage of races that resulted in a podium finish (Top 3), highlighting drivers who consistently performed at the highest level.

paragraphs.title: Driver Consistency Index (Podium Finish Rate)
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ', d.forename,d.surname),'"','') AS driver_name,
    regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','') AS nationality,
    COUNT(*) AS total_races,
    SUM(CASE WHEN r.positionOrder<=3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS podium_finishes,
    ROUND(
        100.0*
        SUM(CASE WHEN r.positionOrder<=3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
        /COUNT(*),2
    ) AS podium_rate
FROM formula1.results r
JOIN formula1.drivers d
ON r.driverId=d.driverId
GROUP BY
regexp_replace(CONCAT_WS(' ',d.forename,d.surname),'"',''),
regexp_replace(d.nationality,'"','')
HAVING COUNT(*)>=50
ORDER BY podium_rate DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
driver_name	nationality	total_races	podium_finishes	podium_rate
Juan Fangio	Argentine	58	35	60.34
Lewis Hamilton	British	356	202	56.74
Max Verstappen	Dutch	209	112	53.59
Alain Prost	French	202	106	52.48
Michael Schumacher	German	308	155	50.32
Ayrton Senna	Brazilian	162	80	49.38
Jim Clark	British	73	32	43.84
Jackie Stewart	British	100	43	43.00
Sebastian Vettel	German	300	122	40.67
Damon Hill	British	122	42	34.43

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Juan Manuel Fangio records the highest podium finish rate in Formula One history, demonstrating extraordinary consistency during the early years of the championship.

Lewis Hamilton, Max Verstappen, Michael Schumacher, Alain Prost, and Ayrton Senna also rank among the most consistent drivers, regularly converting race starts into podium finishes.

Unlike total victories, the podium finish rate measures sustained competitiveness and provides a comprehensive indicator of long-term driving excellence.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Juan Manuel Fangio records the highest podium finish rate in Formula One history, demonstrating extraordinary consistency during the early years of the championship.

Lewis Hamilton, Max Verstappen, Michael Schumacher, Alain Prost, and Ayrton Senna also rank among the most consistent drivers, regularly converting race starts into podium finishes.

Unlike total victories, the podium finish rate measures sustained competitiveness and provides a comprehensive indicator of long-term driving excellence.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🛞 Analytics 12 – Pit Stop Strategy Analysis

## Business Question

Which Formula One circuits require the highest average number of pit stops, and how does this vary across different race venues?

## Objective

This analysis evaluates pit stop frequency across Formula One circuits to identify venues where race strategy and tyre management play the greatest role in determining race outcomes.

msg.data:
🛞 Analytics 12 – Pit Stop Strategy Analysis

Business Question

Which Formula One circuits require the highest average number of pit stops, and how does this vary across different race venues?

Objective

This analysis evaluates pit stop frequency across Formula One circuits to identify venues where race strategy and tyre management play the greatest role in determining race outcomes.

paragraphs.title: Average Pit Stops per Race by Circuit
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    regexp_replace(c.name,'"','') AS circuit_name,
    regexp_replace(c.country,'"','') AS country,
    COUNT(DISTINCT p.raceId) AS races,
    COUNT(*) AS total_pit_stops,
    ROUND(COUNT(*)/COUNT(DISTINCT p.raceId),2) AS avg_pit_stops_per_race
FROM formula1.pit_stops p
JOIN formula1.races r
ON p.raceId=r.raceId
JOIN formula1.circuits c
ON r.circuitId=c.circuitId
GROUP BY
regexp_replace(c.name,'"',''),
regexp_replace(c.country,'"','')
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.raceId)>=5
ORDER BY avg_pit_stops_per_race DESC
LIMIT 10

msg.data:
circuit_name	country	races	total_pit_stops	avg_pit_stops_per_race
Hockenheimring	Germany	5	293	58.6
Autódromo José Carlos Pace	Brazil	13	701	53.92
Sepang International Circuit	Malaysia	7	377	53.86
Shanghai International Circuit	China	10	508	50.8
Bahrain International Circuit	Bahrain	14	686	49.0
Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya	Spain	14	669	47.79
Hungaroring	Hungary	14	667	47.64
Marina Bay Street Circuit	Singapore	12	547	45.58
Suzuka Circuit	Japan	12	536	44.67
Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps	Belgium	13	495	38.08

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

The Hockenheimring records the highest average number of pit stops per race, indicating that tyre degradation and race strategy have historically played a major role at this venue.

Other circuits such as Interlagos, Sepang, Shanghai, and Bahrain also require frequent pit stops due to track characteristics, weather conditions, and tyre management demands.

These findings demonstrate that race strategy varies significantly across Formula One circuits and can strongly influence race outcomes.

msg.data:
Interpretation

The Hockenheimring records the highest average number of pit stops per race, indicating that tyre degradation and race strategy have historically played a major role at this venue.

Other circuits such as Interlagos, Sepang, Shanghai, and Bahrain also require frequent pit stops due to track characteristics, weather conditions, and tyre management demands.

These findings demonstrate that race strategy varies significantly across Formula One circuits and can strongly influence race outcomes.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# ⚡ Analytics 13 – Fastest Lap vs Race Winner

## Business Question

How frequently does the driver who records the fastest lap also win the race?

## Objective

This analysis measures the relationship between fastest lap performance and race victories to determine whether achieving the fastest lap is a reliable indicator of overall race success.

msg.data:
⚡ Analytics 13 – Fastest Lap vs Race Winner

Business Question

How frequently does the driver who records the fastest lap also win the race?

Objective

This analysis measures the relationship between fastest lap performance and race victories to determine whether achieving the fastest lap is a reliable indicator of overall race success.

paragraphs.title: Fastest Lap vs Race Winner
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
'Fastest Lap Winner' AS outcome,
SUM(
CASE
WHEN winner_driverId = fastest_driverId
THEN 1
ELSE 0
END
) AS races
FROM
(
SELECT
raceId,
MAX(CASE WHEN positionOrder=1 THEN driverId END) AS winner_driverId,
MAX(CASE WHEN ranking=1 THEN driverId END) AS fastest_driverId
FROM formula1.results
WHERE ranking IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY raceId
)t

UNION ALL

SELECT
'Fastest Lap Non-Winner',
SUM(
CASE
WHEN winner_driverId <> fastest_driverId
THEN 1
ELSE 0
END
)
FROM
(
SELECT
raceId,
MAX(CASE WHEN positionOrder=1 THEN driverId END) AS winner_driverId,
MAX(CASE WHEN ranking=1 THEN driverId END) AS fastest_driverId
FROM formula1.results
WHERE ranking IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY raceId
)t

msg.data:
outcome	races
Fastest Lap Winner	126
Fastest Lap Non-Winner	284

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Only about one-third of Formula One races are won by the driver who also records the fastest lap.

This finding suggests that while outright speed is important, race victories depend on many additional factors including tyre strategy, fuel management, overtaking opportunities, reliability, weather conditions, and pit stop execution.

Fastest lap therefore reflects peak performance during a race but is not a guarantee of overall victory.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Only about one-third of Formula One races are won by the driver who also records the fastest lap.

This finding suggests that while outright speed is important, race victories depend on many additional factors including tyre strategy, fuel management, overtaking opportunities, reliability, weather conditions, and pit stop execution.

Fastest lap therefore reflects peak performance during a race but is not a guarantee of overall victory.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🏭 Analytics 14 – Constructor Dominance by Decade

## Business Question

Which Formula One constructors dominated each championship decade?

## Objective

This analysis identifies the constructor that accumulated the highest championship points within each decade, illustrating how Formula One team dominance has evolved throughout history.

msg.data:
🏭 Analytics 14 – Constructor Dominance by Decade

Business Question

Which Formula One constructors dominated each championship decade?

Objective

This analysis identifies the constructor that accumulated the highest championship points within each decade, illustrating how Formula One team dominance has evolved throughout history.

paragraphs.title: Constructor Dominance by Decade
paragraphs.text:
%sql

SELECT
    decade,
    constructor,
    total_points
FROM
(
    SELECT
        CONCAT(FLOOR(rac.year/10)*10,'s') AS decade,
        regexp_replace(c.name,'"','') AS constructor,
        ROUND(SUM(res.points),2) AS total_points,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER
        (
            PARTITION BY CONCAT(FLOOR(rac.year/10)*10,'s')
            ORDER BY SUM(res.points) DESC
        ) AS rn

    FROM formula1.results res
    JOIN formula1.races rac
        ON res.raceId = rac.raceId
    JOIN formula1.constructors c
        ON res.constructorId = c.constructorId

    GROUP BY
        CONCAT(FLOOR(rac.year/10)*10,'s'),
        regexp_replace(c.name,'"','')

) t

WHERE rn = 1

ORDER BY decade

msg.data:
decade	constructor	total_points
1950s	Ferrari	754.27
1960s	Ferrari	404.0
1970s	Ferrari	751.5
1980s	McLaren	887.5
1990s	Williams	1121.0
2000s	Ferrari	1737.0
2010s	Mercedes	5112.0
2020s	Red Bull	2948.5

paragraphs.text:
%md

## Interpretation

Ferrari dominated Formula One during the early decades of the championship and re-emerged as the leading constructor throughout the 2000s.

McLaren and Williams led the competitive landscape during the 1980s and 1990s, while Mercedes established unprecedented dominance throughout the hybrid era of the 2010s.

More recently, Red Bull Racing has emerged as the leading constructor of the 2020s, reflecting the continuous evolution of technical innovation and competitive performance in Formula One.

msg.data:
Interpretation

Ferrari dominated Formula One during the early decades of the championship and re-emerged as the leading constructor throughout the 2000s.

McLaren and Williams led the competitive landscape during the 1980s and 1990s, while Mercedes established unprecedented dominance throughout the hybrid era of the 2010s.

# 📊 Analytics 15 – Executive Summary

## Executive Overview

This dashboard presents a comprehensive analysis of the Formula One World Championship (1950–2024) using the Hadoop ecosystem, including HDFS, Apache Hive, and Apache Zeppelin.

Fifteen analytical perspectives were developed to evaluate driver performance, constructor success, championship evolution, race strategy, and historical competitiveness.

The findings provide valuable insights into Formula One performance trends and demonstrate how Big Data technologies can transform large motorsport datasets into meaningful business intelligence.

msg.data:
📊 Analytics 15 – Executive Summary

Executive Overview

This dashboard presents a comprehensive analysis of the Formula One World Championship (1950–2024) using the Hadoop ecosystem, including HDFS, Apache Hive, and Apache Zeppelin.

Fifteen analytical perspectives were developed to evaluate driver performance, constructor success, championship evolution, race strategy, and historical competitiveness.

The findings provide valuable insights into Formula One performance trends and demonstrate how Big Data technologies can transform large motorsport datasets into meaningful business intelligence.

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 🏆 Major Findings

### 🥇 Lewis Hamilton accumulated the highest championship points in Formula One history.

### 🥇 Ferrari remains the highest-scoring constructor across all Formula One seasons.

### 🌍 Italy has hosted the greatest number of Formula One Grands Prix.

### 🏁 Monza is the most frequently used circuit in championship history.

### 🚀 Max Verstappen currently records the highest championship points per race.

### 🎯 Only about 43% of pole positions are converted into race victories.

### ⚡ Only about 31% of fastest laps are achieved by the eventual race winner.

### 🛞 Pit-stop strategies vary significantly between circuits, with Hockenheimring showing the highest average pit-stop frequency.

### 🏭 Formula One constructor dominance has shifted across different eras:
- Ferrari
- McLaren
- Williams
- Mercedes
- Red Bull Racing

msg.data:
🏆 Major Findings

🥇 Lewis Hamilton accumulated the highest championship points in Formula One history.

🥇 Ferrari remains the highest-scoring constructor across all Formula One seasons.

🌍 Italy has hosted the greatest number of Formula One Grands Prix.

🏁 Monza is the most frequently used circuit in championship history.

🚀 Max Verstappen currently records the highest championship points per race.

🎯 Only about 43% of pole positions are converted into race victories.

⚡ Only about 31% of fastest laps are achieved by the eventual race winner.

🛞 Pit-stop strategies vary significantly between circuits, with Hockenheimring showing the highest average pit-stop frequency.

🏭 Formula One constructor dominance has shifted across different eras:

  • Ferrari
  • McLaren
  • Williams
  • Mercedes
  • Red Bull Racing

paragraphs.text:
%md

# 💼 Business Implications

The analytical results demonstrate that Formula One success depends on multiple performance dimensions rather than a single performance indicator.

Although qualifying performance provides a competitive advantage, race victories remain highly dependent on race strategy, tyre management, reliability, and pit-stop execution.

Historical analysis also illustrates the continuous evolution of constructor dominance, reflecting technological innovation and changing competitive landscapes throughout Formula One history.

✔ Apache Hive Data Warehouse

✔ Apache Zeppelin Interactive Analytics

✔ SQL-based Big Data Analytics

✔ Interactive Dashboard Visualization

Dataset:
Formula One World Championship Dataset (1950–2024)

Apache Hadoop Ecosystem

paragraphs.text:
%md

# ✅ Conclusion

This project successfully demonstrates the application of Big Data technologies for analysing large-scale motorsport datasets.

Using Apache Hive and Apache Zeppelin, complex analytical queries were transformed into meaningful visualisations that support strategic decision-making.

The dashboard highlights how data-driven analytics can provide valuable insights into driver performance, constructor competitiveness, championship evolution, and race strategy.

Future work will extend this study using MongoDB and Python to perform predictive analytics and advanced data visualisation.
